methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Karman Industries is a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by David Tearse and Chiranjeev Kalra. The valuation analysis employs triangulation across two applicable methodologies against a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public frontier-tech company in the Climate & Energy sector.

The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $11.5M in total capital raised, yielding an implied valuation of $29M. The Peer-Median method references a set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Watershed ($1.8B Series A), Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A). The median valuation across this set is $175M, though the range spans $110M to $1.8B, reflecting significant dispersion within the peer cohort.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $24M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $102M. The substantial gap between the Stage-Progression result ($29M) and Peer-Median result ($175M) reflects the inherent tension between extrapolating from capital raised versus benchmarking to later-stage peers in a sector marked by variable funding density and exit multiples. Key uncertainties include peer selection sensitivity—particularly the outsized influence of Watershed at $1.8B—and whether stage-adjusted comparables would materially narrow this range.
